untranslatable_reason: This signal requires calculating cross-sectional return deviations from an equal-weighted market index and dynamically sizing positions inversely proportional to those deviations across the entire S&P 500 universe. This is a portfolio-level, relative-value strategy that cannot be expressed as simple indicator-based entry/exit conditions on individual stock prices. Required: (1) daily returns for each stock, (2) equal-weighted market return calculation across all stocks, (3) deviation of each stock from market return, (4) inverse weighting by deviation, (5) market-neutral normalization. The backtest engine must support cross-sectional portfolio construction, not single-instrument signal logic.
translation_notes: CHAN_AT_DRY_5 is a mean-reversion arbitrage strategy operating at the portfolio level with daily rebalancing. It requires a multi-leg position sizing engine and cross-sectional analytics unavailable in the single-instrument rule structure. The 'returns' indicator exists but cannot capture the relative deviation framework needed. Consider implementing this as a separate portfolio construction module rather than a traditional signal rule.
